summary: No-ship: the currency migration is not a complete boot-time data source, so fresh self-migrated environments can come up missing the original 9 currencies despite the committed verification claiming 28-row coverage.

findings.title: Fresh self-migrated databases only get 19 currencies
findings.body: Migration 0008 is the only migration that inserts currency rows, but it inserts only the 19 newly-added codes. Because production boot runs migrations without seedCurrencies, a fresh DB, rebuild, or DR environment created solely from migrations will miss the original 9 currencies (KRW/JPY/VND/TWD/USD/EUR/THB/GBP/CHF). The 28-row verification is masked by the test helper seeding after migrate, so it does not prove the production boot path. Impact: default KRW/JYP-style trip and expense creation can still fail in fresh migrated environments, and recovery depends on an undocumented seed step.
findings.recommendation: Make the data migration insert the full 28-row catalog with ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING, preserving existing 9-row values, or add a baseline seed migration. Add a migration-only test that runs runMigrations without seedCurrencies and asserts all 28 codes/minor_units are present after repeated runs.

next_steps: Patch 0008 or add a follow-up migration so migrations alone produce the full currency catalog.
Add migration-only verification for the 28-row catalog without calling seedCurrencies.
